9618/42/O/N/23 © UCLES 2023 2 An integer is said to be divisible by another integer if the result of the division is also an integer. For example: 10 is divisible by 1, 2, 5 and 10: • 10 ÷ 1 = 10 • 10 ÷ 2 = 5 • 10 ÷ 5 = 2 • 10 ÷ 10 = 1 10 is not divisible by 4: • 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 1, 2, 5 and 10 are said to be the divisors of 10. The iterative function IterativeCalculate() totals all the divisors of its integer parameter and returns this total. Example 1: if the parameter is 10, the total will be 18 (1 + 2 + 5 + 10) . Example 2: if the parameter is 4, the total will be 7 (1 + 2 + 4) . A pseudocode algorithm for IterativeCalculate() is shown. FUNCTION IterativeCalculate(Number : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE Total : Integer DECLARE ToFind : Integer ToFind ← Number Total ← 0 WHILE Number <> 0 IF ToFind MODULUS Number = 0 THEN Total ← Total + Number ENDIF Number ← Number - 1 ENDWHILE RETURN T
